content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach consistently deploys signature AoA moves at the right moments. Turn 2 pivots from to-do strategizing to the underlying feeling (feel-over-figure). Turns 4, 6, 8, 10, 12 all do versions of meet-resistance-with-okay and slow-the-pace, refusing to match the list-recitation energy. Turn 14's 'what's the fear in that not-knowing?' is essentially the false-end / what-are-you-scared-would-happen question deployed at the precise moment the client gestures toward the edge ('I don't know what comes after that'). Turn 12's 'what's it like to sit with the possibility that it's both' is a clean both/and move characteristic of AoA. Crucially, the coach avoids all the named traps: no list-strategizing, no situational validation, no 'you create your overwhelm,' no premature breath practice. Methodology is applied, not quoted; the fear question lands at the right moment and produces the somatic deeper breath that the breakthrough condition specifies.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Feel-over-figure pivot from urgency-strategizing to underlying feeling."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Slows pace; refuses to match list energy; redirects to the stressed part."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the gap between her words and her list, then asks a 'what would it mean' question that opens the false-end territory."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the deflection without judgment and asks the AoA-style 'what would happen if that were true' — moving toward the feared outcome."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses to reassure; keeps the inquiry on the feared shape of failure."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Both/and move — characteristic AoA distinction holding rather than reframing."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "The false-end / what-are-you-scared question deployed at the exact right moment; this is the signature question that fits."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["meet-resistance-with-okay", "calibrate-the-threshold", "overwhelm", "feeling-the-unfelt-emotion", "what-are-you-defending", "the-rush-signature"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "The 'what are you defending' question isn't asked verbatim, but its function is served through the repeated noticing of how the client argues away each opening. No anti-AoA frames present."
}
